codeReview.summary: The storage abstraction is mostly implemented as requested (behaviour, FileStorage wrapper, CloudStorage stubs, and caller refactors), but config-driven CloudStorage selection is not safely handled by all refactored callers.

issues.description: `get_space/1` stores `filled: Storage.exists?(...)` directly in the JSON payload. With `storage_backend` set to `Bropilot.Storage.CloudStorage`, `exists?/3` returns `{:error, :not_implemented}` (see `lib/bropilot/storage/cloud_storage.ex`), and tuple values are not JSON-encodable by Jason. This can raise at response encoding time, so backend switching is not safely supported.

`print_space_status/1` treats `Storage.exists?/3` as a boolean. If CloudStorage is configured, `{:error, :not_implemented}` is truthy and will render slots as filled, producing misleading status output.

`delete/3` uses `File.rm!/1` (bang form), which raises exceptions on filesystem errors instead of returning `{:error, reason}` as the behaviour contract implies. This can crash callers on permission/IO failures.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission AGENTS guidance is now stale: it still instructs workers to use `Bropilot.Map.Store` directly for map IO, which conflicts with this feature's new storage abstraction.
The `elixir-backend` skill still tells workers to use `Bropilot.Map.Store` directly, so skill guidance no longer matches the intended post-feature architecture.

Shared architecture knowledge has not been updated for the abstraction: key modules still present `Bropilot.Map.Store` as the primary map IO module and do not document `Bropilot.Storage` as the interface boundary.

The transcript skeleton shows tests were created and implementation proceeded before any explicit RED-phase failure check, despite the skill requiring a `mix test test/<file>` fail step. This suggests either procedure drift or that skillFeedback may be too lenient in marking procedure adherence.

summary: Reviewed transcript skeleton, handoff, skill, and commit a985ad3. Feature scope is largely delivered, but there is a blocking compatibility bug for config-driven CloudStorage in API payload encoding via `exists?/3`, plus non-blocking robustness/documentation gaps.
